mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in the unemployment rate below 3.5% signals extreme labor market tightness that forces corporate earnings revisions upward, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses below 3.5%, or signal loses predictive power with p > 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with positive returns occurring in >60% of cases, or interaction term IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hike headwinds, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansion predicts negative 20-day returns in >58% of tightening periods, or regime-conditional IC drops below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 290 triggers immediate equity deleveraging as investors anticipate forced central bank rate hikes, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 290, or signal IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending power, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 60-day returns in >55% of observations, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to overall output, which compresses profit margins and yields negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with positive returns exceeding 60% of cases, or interaction IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion during a post-shock recovery phase reflects pent-up demand fulfillment, which boosts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth predicts negative 5-day returns in >58% of recovery periods, or regime-specific IC < 0.02.
